title: Filipin Staining for Cholesterol Visualization in Adherent Cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cfv7m","text":"Filipin staining is a widely used fluorescent method to visualize and quantify unesterified (free) cholesterol in cells and tissues. This protocol describes a reliable and reproducible workflow for performing filipin staining in cultured adherent cells. The procedure includes cell preparation, fixation, permeabilization, and incubation with filipin dye, followed by fluorescence imaging using a UV excitation filter set. Filipin specifically binds to free cholesterol, enabling assessment of cholesterol distribution, membrane organization, and lipid homeostasis under physiological and experimental conditions. This method is particularly useful for studying cholesterol-related pathways, membrane dynamics, and cellular responses to pharmacological manipulation of lipid metabolism. The protocol provides guidance on essential controls, imaging considerations, and troubleshooting to ensure consistent and high-quality results.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"71qpc","text":"1. Avoid using DAPI as a nuclear stain because both filipin and DAPI share similar excitation wavelengths, which can                  lead to signal overlap.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"av976","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"886nk","text":"2. Prepare Fresh Filipin Stock  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bki14","text":"   a. Always make freshly prepared stocks of filipin dye before use.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ev54k","text":"   b. Dissolve filipin in DMSO or the recommended solvent as per vendor instructions.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5a6vk","text":"   c. Protect the stock from light (wrap in foil) and store at -20°C if not used immediately for short duration.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c3aau","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dg31","text":"3. Stock Age  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"m1bb","text":"   a. Prepare a fresh stock (from powder) each time you stain cells.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3f7u0","text":"   b. Do not use stock older than 7–10 days, as filipin degrades and loses fluorescence efficiency.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9osst","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e1gt6","text":"4. Working Solution  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7cv6q","text":"   a. Dilute the stock in the staining buffer/media just before use.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"epjua","text":"   b. Avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles to maintain dye integrity. So, always aliquot they dye in small volumes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dscjd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3aihi","text":"5. Image Acquisition  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2f47d","text":"   a. Acquire images as quickly as possible after staining because filipin fluorescence quenches rapidly.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"54ets","text":"   b. Minimize exposure to light during imaging to reduce photobleaching.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"af787","text":"   c. Use appropriate filter sets for filipin (excitation ~340–380 nm, emission ~385–470 nm).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"846qg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vn3o","text":"6. Additional Notes  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"713av","text":"   a. Optimize staining concentration (commonly 25–50 µg/mL) based on cell type.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"84mvk","text":"   b. Include controls (unstained cells and cholesterol-depleted cells) for validation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f5sj1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"29jsb","text":"7. Seed additional cells on cover slip for negative control (without treatment), positive control and negative secondary antibody control.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"5i1yy","text":"1. Handle filipin with care as it is cytotoxic at high concentrations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Seed 5 × 10⁴ cells (For example 4T1) per well on a coverslip in a 24-well plate and allow them to adhere.

Treat the cells for 04:00:00 with varying concentrations of the desired drug. Such as 2.5 millimolar (mM) Methyl-β-cyclodextrin Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #C4555 to deplete the cholesterol.
Fix the cells with 2 % (v/v) Paraformaldehyde Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #158127 (PFA) for 00:30:00 .
Wash them with 1.5 mg/mL Glycine Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #G8898 in PBS (1X) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) for 00:10:00 at 37 °C to quench the PFA.
Stain the cells with Filipin III Santa Cruz Biotechnology Catalog #sc-205323 50 mg/mL in PBS for 04:00:00 at 37 °C .
Mount the slides with mounting media containing nuclei stain Propidium Iodide (PI) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P4170 2 mg/mL .

Obtain the images using a confocal microscope (for example: Zeiss LSM 880 with a 63× oil immersion objective, 768 × 768 pixels, 20 µm scale bar, and process them using Zeiss ZEN 3.7). Zeiss LSM 700 (Confocal microscope, Zeiss) Zeiss ZEN 3.7 3.7
Perform densitometric analysis using the ImageJ 1.53c Software (National Institute of Health (NIH), USA) and blot the graph showing MFI of Filipin lll against drug concentration. FIJI (Image J) NIH
Remove the media and add the fresh media such as Dulbeccos modified eagle medium (DMEM), high glucose Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #41965062
creator: Himanshi Yaduvanshi
